title: Mars aeolian dunes and planetary sand cycles

shots.speech_text: The key measurement: Bagnold Dunes, Gale Crater: individual dunes up to ~30 m tall, crest-to-crest spacing ~100–300 m; global Mars sand flux 2.2 ± 1.0 m²/yr per meter width (Bridges et al., 2012, Geology). Results like this, produced by NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), transformed our understanding of Mars aeolian dunes and planetary sand cycles.
shots.visual_direction: EVIDENCE SHOT: [Data visualisation, telescope imagery, or archival footage of the key discovery moment]. Institution logos/credits if available. Measurement values as lower-third text overlay.
shots.vo_notes: HELIX voiceover — warm measured female voice, slight British RP accent, inviting and professional — a trusted guide through the unknown, never clinical, wonder without hyperbole

shots.speech_text: Mars aeolian dunes and planetary sand cycles is not just a phenomenon at the far edge of science. It connects directly to questions about the fabric of reality. The e has implications that reach far beyond the laboratory.

shots.vo_notes: HELIX voiceover — warm measured female voice, slight British RP accent, inviting and professional — a trusted guide through the unknown, never clinical, wonder without hyperbole
shots.role_description: Link to the wider universe, human biology, Earth, or the viewer's daily experience

shots.speech_text: We are only beginning to understand Mars aeolian dunes and planetary sand cycles. And perhaps that's exactly the point — the universe keeps its most intricate secrets just within reach, if we keep looking.

provenance_card.key_measurement: Bagnold Dunes, Gale Crater: individual dunes up to ~30 m tall, crest-to-crest spacing ~100–300 m; global Mars sand flux 2.2 ± 1.0 m²/yr per meter width (Bridges et al., 2012, Geology)

provenance_card.accuracy_note: All measurements cited. Uncertainty stated where present. No fabricated statistics.

guardrails: HELIX is voiceover only — no on-screen host, no synthetic presenter, no avatar.
All quantitative claims must include units and citation.
Uncertainty must be stated where present (e.g. 'approximately', 'within a margin of').
Do not present theoretical models as confirmed fact without qualification.
Do not fabricate measurements, paper titles, DOIs, or institutional affiliations.
Maintain wonder without hyperbole — the phenomena are extraordinary enough.
No speculation presented as established science.
HELIX does not cover history, biography, or language — those are DAVID's domain.
